items.title: USDA posts updated SNAP food-restriction waiver tracker: 22 states approved, with many implementation dates in 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP food-restriction waiver page lists 22 states with approved demonstration waivers that restrict purchases of items like soda and candy. The tracker includes multiple 2026 target implementation dates—for example: Utah, Indiana, Iowa, Nebraska, and West Virginia (Jan. 1, 2026); Texas (Apr. 1, 2026); Florida (Apr. 20, 2026); and Missouri (Oct. 1, 2026).

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS says states must implement a statutory change starting October 1, 2026 that limits when states can claim federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens. CMS notes the change does not affect federal matching funds for emergency medical conditions and does not change states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: IRS reminder: EITC refunds can’t be issued before Feb. 21, 2026; most direct deposits expected by March 2
items.summary: IRS says it must wait until at least Feb. 21, 2026 to issue refunds for returns claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC). If you e-file, choose direct deposit, and your return has no issues, IRS says most EITC refunds are expected by March 2, 2026; IRS also reports the average EITC amount for tax year 2024 was $2,916.

items.title: USDA publishes 2026 Summer EBT (SUN Bucks) benefit levels: $40/month in most states; higher amounts for Alaska, Hawaii, and territories
items.summary: USDA says 2026 Summer EBT benefits remain $40 per child per month (total $120 for summer) in the 48 states and D.C., effective Jan. 1–Dec. 31, 2026. USDA’s 2026 table lists Alaska benefits of $54/month (Urban), $69/month (Rural 1), and $84/month (Rural 2), plus Hawaii at $63/month and participating U.S. territories at $60/month.

items.title: USDA WIC memo sets FY 2026 fruit-and-vegetable cash-value benefit amounts; states must allow at least one non-fresh option by April 2026
items.summary: USDA’s FY 2026 WIC cash-value benefit memo lists monthly amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants (FY 2026 runs Oct. 1, 2025–Sept. 30, 2026). USDA also notes that WIC food package changes require states to authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: HUD rescinds documentation flexibilities for Emergency Housing Vouchers and Stability Vouchers; PHAs must verify SSNs and eligible immigration status
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-05 removes the option that let PHAs admit some Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) families without verifying Social Security numbers and eligible immigration/citizenship documentation up front (with a 180-day follow-up window). HUD directs PHAs to follow standard verification regulations and says they must be in compliance within 60 days of publication for new SV admissions and current EHV/SV families.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; higher payments start in January 2026 (and Dec. 31, 2025 for SSI)
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026, affecting Social Security (OASDI) and SSI benefits. SSA says increased payments begin in January 2026 for nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries and on Dec. 31, 2025 for nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients; SSA also says the taxable maximum rises to $184,500.
